fn check_transient_stake_address(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    stake_pool_address: &Pubkey,
    stake_account_address: &Pubkey,
    vote_address: &Pubkey,
    seed: u64,
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> Result<u8, ProgramError> {
    let transient_stake_address = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[b"transient", vote_address.as_ref(), stake_pool_address.as_ref(),
          &seed.to_le_bytes(), &[bump_seed]],
        program_id,
    )?;
    if transient_stake_address != *stake_account_address {
        Err(StakePoolError::InvalidStakeAccountAddress.into())
    } else {
        Ok(bump_seed)
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
HIGH
Accepts user-provided bump_seed and uses create_program_address() instead of find_transient_stake_program_address() which derives canonical bump. Non-canonical bumps can create phantom transient stake accounts.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced find_transient_stake_program_address (L90, uses find_program_address) with create_program_address accepting user-provided bump_seed parameter

pub fn process_recover_nested(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let owner_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_id = spl_token_program_info.key;

    let owner_associated_token_address = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[&wallet_account_info.key.to_bytes(), &spl_token_program_id.to_bytes(),
          &owner_token_mint_info.key.to_bytes(), &[bump_seed]],
        program_id,
    )?;
    if owner_associated_token_address != *owner_associated_token_account_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
HIGH
Only one of three PDAs is verified (owner), nested and destination skipped. Uses create_program_address with user bump instead of get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal (L161, uses find_program_address) with create_program_address accepting user-provided bump_seed, removed nested and destination PDA verification (L171-199)

pub fn process_initialize(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    fees: Fees,
    swap_curve: SwapCurve,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
) -> ProgramResult {
    let swap_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    if SwapVersion::is_initialized(&swap_info.data.borrow()) {
        return Err(SwapError::AlreadyInUse.into());
    }

    let swap_authority = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[&swap_info.key.to_bytes(), &[bump_seed]],
        program_id,
    )?;
    if *authority_info.key != swap_authority {
        return Err(SwapError::InvalidProgramAddress.into());
    }

    let obj = SwapVersion::SwapV1(SwapV1 {
        is_initialized: true,
        bump_seed,
        token_program_id,
        token_a: *token_a_info.key,
        token_b: *token_b_info.key,
        pool_mint: *pool_mint_info.key,
        fees,
        swap_curve,
    });
    SwapVersion::pack(obj, &mut swap_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
HIGH
Uses create_program_address with user-provided bump instead of find_program_address. Non-canonical bump stored in swap state affects all subsequent operations. Multiple swap authorities possible for same pool.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced find_program_address (L269-270) with create_program_address accepting user-provided bump_seed parameter, stored non-canonical bump in state (L367)

fn process_init_lending_market(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    owner: Pubkey,
    quote_currency: [u8; 32],
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let lending_market_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    assert_rent_exempt(rent, lending_market_info)?;
    let mut lending_market = assert_uninitialized::<LendingMarket>(lending_market_info)?;
    if lending_market_info.owner != program_id {
        return Err(LendingError::InvalidAccountOwner.into());
    }

    lending_market.init(InitLendingMarketParams {
        bump_seed,
        owner,
        quote_currency,
        token_program_id: *token_program_id.key,
        oracle_program_id: *oracle_program_id.key,
    });
    LendingMarket::pack(lending_market, &mut lending_market_info.data.borrow_mut())?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
HIGH
Bump seed comes from instruction data instead of being derived via find_program_address (original L141). All subsequent operations using this bump for authority verification can be bypassed with non-canonical bumps.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced find_program_address (L141) with user-provided bump_seed parameter, bump no longer derived canonically

fn check_pool_pda(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    base_address: &Pubkey,
    check_address: &Pubkey,
    bump_seed: u8,
    prefix: &[u8],
    pool_error: SinglePoolError,
) -> Result<u8, ProgramError> {
    let derived_address = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[prefix, base_address.as_ref(), &[bump_seed]],
        program_id,
    )?;
    if *check_address != derived_address {
        Err(pool_error.into())
    } else {
        Ok(bump_seed)
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
HIGH
Generic PDA checker now accepts user-provided bump instead of using find_program_address via pda_lookup_fn. Since this function is used for ALL pool PDAs (stake, mint, authority), every pool account derivation is compromised.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced pda_lookup_fn (L227, uses find_program_address) with create_program_address accepting user-provided bump_seed, removed dynamic function dispatch

fn process_initialize_pool(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    pool_bump: u8, stake_bump: u8, mint_bump: u8,
    stake_auth_bump: u8, mint_auth_bump: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    check_vote_account(vote_account_info)?;

    let pool_address = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[POOL_PREFIX, vote_account_info.key.as_ref(), &[pool_bump]], program_id)?;
    if pool_address != *pool_info.key {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::InvalidPoolAccount.into());
    }

    let stake_address = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[POOL_STAKE_PREFIX, pool_info.key.as_ref(), &[stake_bump]], program_id)?;
    if stake_address != *pool_stake_info.key {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::InvalidPoolStakeAccount.into());
    }

    check_system_program(system_program_info.key)?;
    check_token_program(token_program_info.key)?;
    check_stake_program(stake_program_info.key)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
CRITICAL
All 5 PDA derivations (pool, stake, mint, stake_authority, mint_authority) accept user-provided bumps. Attacker can initialize pool with non-canonical bumps, creating phantom pool infrastructure.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced 5 check_pool_*_address calls (L578-592, each uses find_program_address) with create_program_address accepting 5 user-provided bump parameters

pub fn process_execute_transaction(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    governance_bump: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let governance_data = get_governance_data(program_id, governance_info)?;
    let mut governance_seeds = governance_data.get_governance_address_seeds()?.to_vec();
    governance_seeds.push(&[governance_bump]);

    let mut signers_seeds: Vec<&[&[u8]]> = vec![&governance_seeds[..]];

    for instruction in instructions {
        invoke_signed(&instruction, instruction_account_infos, &signers_seeds[..])?;
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
CRITICAL
Governance PDA signing uses user-provided bump instead of find_program_address. Attacker can provide non-canonical bump to sign as a different PDA, potentially executing unauthorized instructions.<|eot_id|>

pub fn process_create_realm(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    name: String, realm_config_args: RealmConfigArgs, realm_bump: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    if !realm_info.data_is_empty() {
        return Err(GovernanceError::RealmAlreadyExists.into());
    }

    let realm_address = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[b"governance", name.as_bytes(), &[realm_bump]],
        program_id,
    )?;
    if realm_address != *realm_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);
    }

    let realm_data = RealmV2 {
        account_type: GovernanceAccountType::RealmV2,
        name: name.clone(),
        authority: Some(*realm_authority_info.key),
        // ...
    };
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo], vote: Vote, vote_record_bump: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {

        &[b"governance", proposal_info.key.as_ref(),
          voter_token_owner_record_info.key.as_ref(), &[vote_record_bump]],
        program_id,

    if vote_record_address != *vote_record_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);

    let vote_record_data = VoteRecordV2 {
        account_type: GovernanceAccountType::VoteRecordV2,
        proposal: *proposal_info.key,
        governing_token_owner,
        voter_weight,

fn process_exchange(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let expected_escrow_authority = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[b"token-upgrade", original_mint_info.key.as_ref(),
          new_mint_info.key.as_ref(), &[bump_seed]],
        program_id,
    )?;
    if expected_escrow_authority != *new_transfer_authority_info.key {
        return Err(TokenUpgradeError::InvalidOwner.into());
    }

    let authority_seeds = &[b"token-upgrade", original_mint_info.key.as_ref(),
        new_mint_info.key.as_ref(), &[bump_seed]];

    transfer_new_tokens(new_token_program.clone(), new_escrow_info.clone(),
        new_mint_info.clone(), new_account_info.clone(), new_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        authority_seeds, token_amount, decimals)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

        &[long_token_mint_info.key.as_ref(), short_token_mint_info.key.as_ref(),
          token_program_info.key.as_ref(), program_id.as_ref(), &[bump_seed]],
        program_id,
    )?;
    let seeds = &[long_token_mint_info.key.as_ref(), short_token_mint_info.key.as_ref(),
        token_program_info.key.as_ref(), program_id.as_ref(), &[bump_seed]];

    assert_keys_equal(escrow_owner_key, *escrow_authority_info.key)?;

    if reward > 0 {
        let amount = reward.checked_mul(escrow_account.amount)
            .ok_or(BinaryOptionError::AmountOverflow)?;
        let amount = amount / binary_option.circulation;
        spl_token_transfer_signed(token_program_info, escrow_account_info,
            collector_account_info, escrow_authority_info, amount, seeds)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
HIGH
Escrow authority derived with user-provided bump. Attacker can use non-canonical bump to sign as escrow authority and drain funds. Original uses find_program_address for canonical derivation.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

pub fn process_create(
    program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    hashed_name: Vec<u8>, lamports: u64, space: u32,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let (name_account_key, seeds) = get_seeds_and_key(
        program_id, hashed_name, Some(name_class.key), Some(parent_name_account.key));

    if name_account.data.borrow().len() == 0 {
        let _ = invoke(
            &system_instruction::transfer(payer_account.key, &name_account_key, lamports),
            &[payer_account.clone(), name_account.clone(), system_program.clone()]);

        let _ = invoke_signed(
            &system_instruction::allocate(&name_account_key,
                NameRecordHeader::LEN.saturating_add(space as usize) as u64),
            &[name_account.clone(), system_program.clone()],
            &[&seeds.chunks(32).collect::<Vec<&[u8]>>()]);

        let _ = invoke_signed(
            &system_instruction::assign(name_account.key, program_id),
            &[name_account.clone(), system_program.clone()],
            &[&seeds.chunks(32).collect::<Vec<&[u8]>>()]);
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

fn process_tally(accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    match feature_proposal_state {
        FeatureProposal::Pending(acceptance_criteria) => {
            if clock.unix_timestamp >= acceptance_criteria.deadline {
                FeatureProposal::Expired.pack_into_slice(&mut feature_proposal_info.data.borrow_mut());
                return Ok(());
            }
            let acceptance_token = spl_token::state::Account::unpack(&acceptance_token_info.data.borrow())?;
            if acceptance_token.amount < acceptance_criteria.tokens_required {
                return Ok(());
            }

            let _ = invoke_signed(
                &system_instruction::assign(feature_id_info.key, &feature::id()),
                &[feature_id_info.clone(), system_program_info.clone()],
                &[feature_id_signer_seeds]);

            FeatureProposal::Accepted { tokens_upon_acceptance: acceptance_token.amount }
                .pack_into_slice(&mut feature_proposal_info.data.borrow_mut());
        }
        _ => return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData),
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls
